title: Can existing Monday items be linked to existing Jira issues?
excerpt: The current integration between Monday and Jira only works if Monday items or Jira issue doesn’t exist. Then if you actualize/create a Monday item it will create a new Jira issue, and vice versa. After this link is creat…

posts.author: idelviso (Ivan)
posts.cookedHtml:
The current integration between Monday and Jira only works if Monday items or Jira issue doesn’t exist. Then if you actualize/create a Monday item it will create a new Jira issue, and vice versa. After this link is created all changes will be actualized in the other application without problems.
But it’s impossible to link an existing Jira issue with an existing Monday item.

Could you create this integration? or update the existing integration to let this work?

Thanks in advance.

posts.author: YuleyEldar (Yuley Eldar)
posts.cookedHtml:
I would very much like to have this option as well!
especially considering the fact that I’m trying to migrate my boards from the old integration to the new one, and all items are already created on both platforms.

posts.author: jeanms (Jean Sandberg)
posts.cookedHtml: We must have this feature in order for the Jira-Monday integration to be useful.

posts.author: vrvora (Viral Vora)
posts.cookedHtml: We must have this feature in order for the Jira-Monday integration to be useful.

posts.author: JamesClem (James Clem)
posts.cookedHtml:
Linking this gem I found today- perhaps this helps?

Pre-existing JIRA Integration Platform discussions
Hi Connor, I came up against the same limitation for a similar problem so I used a Post Function with the JIRA add-on ScriptRunner to perform an API call that creates a pulse when a particular state change occurs (see details - Using the API to create a pulse with JIRA ticket details from a JIRA state change - #4 by AlexSavchuk) I know this means you’d have to do a state change to get them in so if that’s not appropriate then there’s a script testing console that you could use to get the items …
